name: Champagne Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. If using optional ingredients, see instructions at the end.
2. Cut chicken into 1/4-inch wide slices.
3. Melt butter and olive oil in a large heavy skillet.
4. Saute the chicken for 4 to 5 minutes until browned; remove and keep warm.
5. Deglaze the pan with champagne, then add cream. Stir in tarragon and reduce sauce for two minutes.
6. Return chicken to the pan, bring just to a simmer.
7. Remove to serving platter; serve hot with fresh vegetables or over rice or pasta (e.g., fettucini).
8. Pound each chicken half between sheets of waxed paper until about 1/8-inch thick.
9. Saute shallots in butter until tender, then divide and place evenly on each breast half.
10. Place a slice of prosciutto on each breast; fold over and secure with toothpicks.
11. Cook the breasts for another 4 to 5 minutes on each side in the remaining butter and olive oil.
12. Chicken is done when it springs back slightly when pressed with a finger.
13. Follow the balance of instructions above for preparing the sauce.
